semantics: Voice cards where three independent blind passes disagreed by 2 or more points on the voice-concealment axis. These are the cards whose text does not pin down how the performance actually sounds; they need a listener, not another scorer.
method: 3 independent haiku passes, filed value withheld. Cards agreeing within one point were filed at the median; these did not.

rows.basis: Darth Vader's iconic voice has signature processing and vocal effects; substantially altered.
June Foray's voice is legendary and immediately recognizable. Her instrument is the standard. Characters performed in own voice.
Homer iconic stylized character audiences recognize; 'erasing himself into cartoon' suggests substantial processing/alteration.
Explicitly 'synthetic tones'—electronic processing creates artificial AI voice from natural voice.
Dramatic lead voices serene alien far outside his register, but uses character work not processing.
Deep gravel-voiced specialist; his natural instrument applied to growling character.
Earth-shaking roar emphasises vocal distortion across iconic roles; substantially altered.

rows.production: Star Wars video games / Vader Immortal
rows.basis: Vader growl designed to evoke James Earl Jones without being him. Substantially altered register and character.
Young Jedi on preschool show. Register shift within acting range, not extreme concealment.
Voice-matching Donald Glover to create the illusion of another actor performing the role.
Channels Carrie Fisher—light vocal impression or homage, not radical alteration. Lightly characterised.
Baby and superhero; sustained character voices far outside natural register, likely pitch-altered for infant character.
Creature characters (alien Jedi, fish-headed admiral). Far outside register, sustained.
Kenny's voice muffled 'beyond words entirely' by parka—consistent, substantial obstruction of intelligibility.

rows.character: Tony the Tiger & the Grinch's song
rows.production: Kellogg's / How the Grinch Stole Christmas
rows.basis: Distinctive cavernous bass is his natural signature voice. Advertising delivery and song are inflected, not processed.
Backus's Mr. Magoo uses character register and inflection within his own instrument (comparable to Batman anchor).
Kasem's Shaggy uses character inflection and register shift, remains recognizable as his voice work (similar to Batman anchor).
Courtly robotic voice—sustained machine character far outside natural register, substantially altered.
Growled brute voice and radio announcer. 'Growl' indicates vocal processing/technique, substantially altered from conversational baseline.
Two human characters (Leonardo, Liquid Snake). No processing mentioned. Different roles but lightly characterised within his own vocal instrument.
Dramatic actor voices a bear (non-human) requiring vocal characterisation outside his acting register.
Disembodied brain-creature. Substantially altered; alien voice far outside normal register.
Secombe's 'wheezy' Toydarian is character affectation far from dramatic acting, but uses instrument not processing.
Menacing character voice but recognizable as Jackson's instrument; the actor is known for this role.
Sleepy mumble is very distinctive sustained vocal delivery. Clear acting choice, within his range.
Control Voice 'calm menace'—deliberate narrator register; his voice in mode, no processing.
Goofy's register shift and inflection within Farmer's recognizable instrument. Lightly altered sustained character voice.
Boy-hero character voice with high energy held 40+ years; clearly characterized, but within her vocal instrument.
Booming announcer voice applied to robot; his natural instrument is the signature.
Giant heated-table monster voice. Character-specific vocalization. Likely adapted with attitude.
Alien overlord voice in different register from known hero roles; character work but no processing.
Voice actor performs monster character within his range; no processing indicated.
'Whirring duke' of Org horde. Mechanical descriptor suggests substantial vocal alteration for non-human creature, far outside human baseline.
Barbaric Machine Beast (Kaiju). Veteran repeatedly voices Sentai monsters—monster roles require substantial vocal alteration from human baseline.
Voice actor performs Camera Org as character voice within his range.
Character voice for Furaimaru within Miyata's vocal range; no processing indicated.
Machine-beast creature; seiyuu 'buried' inside kaiju suggests register/character substantially removed from natural.
Noodle-obsessed character slurping through forms. Stylised character voice with affectations; no processing evident.
Booming comic voice with growl inflection; Anzai's natural instrument pushed for monster character.
Voice actor known for Alucard performs tyrant character within his vocal range.
Shaughnessy's lisping fox-terrier knight uses speech affectation and character inflection; lightly characterized.
Sustained young boy character voiced by adult woman. Far outside natural register.
Snarling fish-man character voice. Distinctly characterized for creature role. His instrument.
Full cast with Hutts, officers, Thrawn are register shifts without processing. Lightly characterised voices.
Broadway tenor in Sith Inquisitor; character inflection over trained vocal instrument, no disguise.
Goofy's 'gawrsh' is register shift within Pope's instrument. Lightly altered sustained character voice.
